title: satureja vulgaris subsp. nepeta
intro: Satureja vulgaris subsp. nepeta, commonly known as Nepeta-like savory, is a subspecies of the savory plant (Satureja vulgaris) native to the Mediterranean region. It belongs to the Lamiaceae family, known for its aromatic and medicinal properties. This plant has been traditionally used for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The leaves and flowers of Satureja vulgaris subsp. nepeta can be used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular natural remedy for various health benefits, including reducing stress, improving sleep, and supporting immune function.
